title: Wood-fired, Creamy Dutch Scamorza Bake
description: A rich, creamy Dutch one-pot dish featuring scamorza cheese, perfect for a cold winter evening. The wood-fired flavor adds a unique twist to this comforting meal.

ingredients.name: 1 cup Dutch white wine
ingredients.notes: dry red wine can be substituted

instructions: Preheat the oven to 425°F (220°C) and the wood-fired grill to medium-high heat.
In a large Dutch oven or oven-safe pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the sliced scamorza cheese and cook, stirring occasionally, until the cheese starts to melt and bubble.
Add the Dutch white wine, scraping the bottom of the pot to release any browned bits. Bring the wine to a simmer and cook until reduced by half, about 5 minutes.
Transfer the pot to the preheated wood-fired grill and cook, covered, for 10-12 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and bake for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until the cheese is golden brown and the flavors have melded together.
Remove the pot from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving hot.
